chapter_title.en: Chapter on Rare Cases of Bequests

text: 5558 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَمْزَةَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ فِي بَلَدِنَا رُبَّمَا أُوصِيَ بِالْمَالِ، لآِلِ مُحَمَّدٍ فَيَأْتُونِّي بِهِ فَأَكْرَهُ أَنْ أَحْمِلَهُ إِلَيْكَ حَتَّى أَسْتَأْمِرَكَ فَقَالَ "لاَ تَأْتِنِي بِهِ وَ لاَ تَعَرَّضْ لَهُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5558 - Ali ibn Mahziyar narrated from Ahmad ibn Hamzah, who said:
I said to Imam (as): 'In our town, sometimes people bequeath wealth to the family of Muhammad (sw), and they bring it to me. However, I hesitate to deliver it to you until I seek your permission.'
Imam (as) replied: "Do not bring it to me, and do not involve yourself with it."
